name: 0.91" 128×32 OLED Display Module (SSD1306, I2C)
description: 0.91 inch monochrome OLED display module, 128×32 pixel resolution, SSD1306 driver, I2C interface (address 0x3C default), 3.3-5V operating voltage, 22.38×5.58mm active area, 4-pin header (GND/VCC/SCL/SDA), white/blue/yellow variants, ultra-compact form factor for status readouts
designNotes: Smallest common OLED — good for single-line status displays, small dashboards. Use Adafruit SSD1306 or U8g2 library. I2C address is 0x3C by default; some modules have solder jumper for 0x3D to allow two displays on one bus. Pin order varies by manufacturer (some swap GND/VCC) — always verify before wiring. No mounting holes on most modules — use double-sided tape, adhesive standoffs, or design a clip/cradle. 3.3V logic native but most modules include onboard regulator for 5V MCUs. Very low current draw makes it ideal for battery projects.

name: 0.96" 128×64 OLED Display Module (SSD1306, I2C)
description: 0.96 inch monochrome OLED display module, 128×64 pixel resolution, SSD1306 driver, I2C interface (address 0x3C default), 3.3-5V operating voltage, 21.74×10.86mm active area, 4-pin header (GND/VCC/SCL/SDA), white/blue/yellow-blue dual-color variants, most popular small OLED for Arduino and ESP32 projects
designNotes: The most popular small OLED display for maker projects. Use Adafruit SSD1306 library or U8g2. I2C address 0x3C default; solder jumper on some boards selects 0x3D for dual-display setups. Pin order is NOT standardized — verify GND/VCC order on your specific module before wiring. Adafruit version has mounting holes; most generic modules do not. Works great with ESP32, Arduino Nano, Raspberry Pi Pico. Some generic modules come in yellow-blue dual-color variant (top 16 rows yellow, bottom 48 rows blue — not software controllable, it's the OLED material). For SPI version (faster updates), see SPI variant.

name: 0.96" 128×64 OLED Display Module (SSD1306, SPI)
description: 0.96 inch monochrome OLED display module, 128×64 pixel resolution, SSD1306 driver, SPI interface (4-wire with DC pin), 3.3-5V operating voltage, 21.74×10.86mm active area, 7-pin header (GND/VCC/D0/D1/RES/DC/CS), faster refresh than I2C variant, up to 10MHz SPI clock

specs.pinout: GND, VCC, D0(SCK), D1(MOSI), RES, DC, CS

electrical.pinout: 7-pin: GND, VCC, D0(SCK), D1(MOSI), RES, DC, CS

designNotes: SPI variant of the popular 0.96" OLED — faster screen updates than I2C at the cost of more GPIO pins (7 vs 4). Use when refresh rate matters (animations, fast-updating data). Adafruit 326 supports both I2C and SPI via solder jumper. SPI clock up to 10MHz. Uses more GPIO than I2C — consider I2C version if pins are scarce. Same libraries work (Adafruit SSD1306, U8g2) with SPI constructor.

name: 1.3" 128×64 OLED Display Module (SH1106, I2C)
description: 1.3 inch monochrome OLED display module, 128×64 pixel resolution, SH1106 driver chip, I2C interface (address 0x3C), 3.3-5V operating voltage, 29.42×14.70mm active area, 4-pin header, white or blue variants, larger readable display area than 0.96 inch OLED, 132×64 internal RAM with 2-column offset
designNotes: Larger and more readable than 0.96" OLED at the same 128×64 resolution. IMPORTANT: SH1106 is NOT the same as SSD1306 — it has 132×64 internal RAM with a 2-column offset, so SSD1306 libraries will show shifted content. Use U8g2 (with SH1106 constructor) or Adafruit SH110X library. Some 1.3" modules are mislabeled and actually contain SSD1306 — test with both libraries if display looks wrong. Most generic modules have 2 mounting holes at diagonal corners. Good upgrade path from 0.96" OLED when readability is needed without changing code much.

name: 1.3" 128×64 OLED Display Module (SH1106, SPI)
description: 1.3 inch monochrome OLED display module, 128×64 pixel resolution, SH1106 driver chip, SPI interface (4-wire with DC pin), 3.3-5V operating voltage, 29.42×14.70mm active area, 7-pin header (GND/VCC/CLK/MOSI/RES/DC/CS), faster refresh than I2C variant, up to 8MHz SPI clock

specs.pinout: GND, VCC, CLK, MOSI, RES, DC, CS

electrical.pinout: 7-pin: GND, VCC, CLK, MOSI, RES, DC, CS

designNotes: SPI variant of the 1.3" SH1106 OLED — faster updates than I2C at cost of more pins. Same driver chip caveats apply: SH1106 has 132×64 RAM with 2-column offset, must use SH1106-specific library or constructor. Less common than the I2C version. SPI clock up to ~8MHz. Use U8g2 with SH1106 SPI constructor or Adafruit SH110X library with SPI.

name: 16×2 Character LCD with I2C Backpack (HD44780 + PCF8574)
description: 16 character × 2 line character LCD module with I2C backpack, HD44780 compatible controller, PCF8574T I2C expander (address 0x27 or 0x3F), 5V operation, 80×36mm PCB, 64.5×16mm viewable area, 5×8 dot matrix characters, adjustable contrast potentiometer, LED backlight (green-yellow or blue-white), 4 mounting holes on 75×31mm pattern

electrical.pinout: 4-pin via backpack: GND, VCC, SDA, SCL

designNotes: The classic Arduino display. I2C backpack reduces wiring from 12+ wires to 4 (GND/VCC/SDA/SCL). Requires 5V — most 3.3V MCUs need level shifting or a 5V supply. I2C address is usually 0x27 (PCF8574T) but some modules use 0x3F (PCF8574AT) — run I2C scanner if display doesn't respond. Contrast potentiometer on backpack must be adjusted physically — if display appears blank, turn the pot. Use LiquidCrystal_I2C library for Arduino. Four M2.5 mounting holes at corners (75×31mm spacing). Backlight draws significant current — can be disabled via solder jumper on backpack for battery projects. Not suitable for 3.3V-only systems without level shifting.

name: 20×4 Character LCD with I2C Backpack (HD44780 + PCF8574)
description: 20 character × 4 line character LCD module with I2C backpack, HD44780 compatible controller, PCF8574T I2C expander (address 0x27 or 0x3F), 5V operation, 98×60mm PCB, 77×25.2mm viewable area, 5×8 dot matrix characters, adjustable contrast potentiometer, LED backlight, 4 mounting holes on 93×55mm pattern

electrical.pinout: 4-pin via backpack: GND, VCC, SDA, SCL

designNotes: Larger version of the 16×2 LCD — 4 lines of 20 characters allows for menus, status dashboards, and data logging displays. Same I2C backpack and library as 16×2 (LiquidCrystal_I2C). Requires 5V. Address detection: run I2C scanner sketch. Backlight draws significant current (~100mA). Mount with M2.5 screws through corner holes (93×55mm pattern). Line addressing is non-contiguous: line 0 starts at 0x00, line 1 at 0x40, line 2 at 0x14, line 3 at 0x54 — most libraries handle this automatically. Good for enclosure front panels — standard industry size with well-defined mounting.

name: 1.8" 128×160 TFT Display Module (ST7735, SPI)
description: 1.8 inch color TFT LCD display module, 128×160 pixel resolution, 65K colors (RGB565), ST7735S/ST7735R driver chip, SPI interface with DC pin, 3.3-5V operating voltage, 28.03×35.04mm active area, 8 or 11 pin header (some include SD card slot), LED backlight with PWM control, compact full-color display for embedded projects

specs.pinout: GND, VCC, SCL, SDA, RES, DC, CS, BLK

electrical.pinout: 8-pin: GND, VCC, SCL, SDA(MOSI), RES, DC, CS, BLK

designNotes: The smallest practical full-color TFT. Use Adafruit ST7735 library or TFT_eSPI (faster, more configurable). ST7735 has multiple sub-variants (ST7735S, ST7735R, green/black/red tab) — wrong initialization causes offset or inverted colors. TFT_eSPI lets you configure the exact variant. SPI clock up to 15-20MHz for fast updates. Backlight pin (BLK) can be PWM-controlled for brightness adjustment or tied to VCC for always-on. Some modules include microSD card slot (additional pins for MISO, SD_CS). Good balance of size, color capability, and pin count. Pairs well with ESP32 or Arduino Nano for compact color UI.

name: 2.4" 240×320 TFT Display Module (ILI9341, SPI)
description: 2.4 inch color TFT LCD display module, 240×320 pixel resolution (QVGA), 262K colors, ILI9341 driver chip, SPI interface with DC pin, 3.3-5V operating voltage, 36.72×48.96mm active area, 9-14 pin header, optional XPT2046 resistive touchscreen, LED backlight, popular mid-size color display for dashboards and GUIs

specs.pinout: VCC, GND, CS, RESET, DC, SDI(MOSI), SCK, LED, SDO(MISO)

electrical.pinout: 9-pin display + 5-pin touch (T_CLK, T_CS, T_DIN, T_DO, T_IRQ)

designNotes: The workhorse mid-size TFT — good resolution, fast SPI, great library support. Use TFT_eSPI library for best performance (DMA support on ESP32). ILI9341 supports RGB565 over SPI (unlike ILI9488), making it faster than the 3.5" version for full-screen updates. Touch versions include XPT2046 controller on separate SPI — shares MOSI/SCK but needs its own CS pin. Needs 6+ GPIO minimum (CS, DC, RST, MOSI, SCK, LED) plus 2 more for touch (T_CS, T_IRQ). ESP32 is the ideal MCU pairing — enough pins and fast SPI. Full screen refresh at 10MHz SPI takes ~50ms. For LVGL GUI framework, this is the recommended display size.

name: 3.5" 320×480 TFT Display Module (ILI9488, SPI)
description: 3.5 inch color TFT LCD display module, 320×480 pixel resolution (HVGA), 262K colors (RGB666), ILI9488 driver chip, SPI interface, 3.3-5V operating voltage, 48.96×73.44mm active area, optional XPT2046 resistive touchscreen, LED backlight, large color display for embedded GUIs and dashboards, RGB666 required over SPI (no RGB565 support)

specs.pinout: VCC, GND, CS, RESET, DC, MOSI, SCK, LED, MISO + touch pins

electrical.pinout: 9-pin display + 5-pin touch (T_CLK, T_CS, T_DIN, T_DO, T_IRQ)

designNotes: CRITICAL: ILI9488 does NOT support RGB565 (16-bit) over SPI — it requires RGB666 (18-bit, 3 bytes per pixel). This makes SPI communication ~33% slower than the 2.4" ILI9341 for full-screen updates despite the higher max SPI clock. Full screen refresh is noticeably slower. For fast updates, consider 8-bit parallel interface (shield form factor) or the 2.4" ILI9341 instead. TFT_eSPI library supports ILI9488 but must be configured for RGB666. Adafruit 2050 actually uses HX8357D driver (not ILI9488) — verify your specific module's driver chip. Backlight draws significant current (~100-150mA). Best paired with ESP32-S3 for sufficient pins and DMA SPI. Good for LVGL-based touch GUIs where readability matters more than refresh speed.

name: 1.54" 200×200 E-Paper Display Module (SPI, Waveshare Compatible)
description: 1.54 inch e-paper (e-ink) display module, 200×200 pixel resolution, bi-color black/white, SSD1681 driver chip, SPI interface with BUSY pin, 3.3V operating voltage, 27.6×27.6mm active area, 8-pin header (VCC/GND/DIN/CLK/CS/DC/RST/BUSY), ultra-low power static display, ~2 second full refresh, partial refresh supported, 180° viewing angle, sunlight readable

specs.pinout: VCC, GND, DIN(MOSI), CLK(SCK), CS, DC, RST, BUSY

electrical.pinout: 8-pin: VCC, GND, DIN, CLK, CS, DC, RST, BUSY

designNotes: E-paper retains image with zero power — ideal for battery-powered displays, price tags, badges. BUSY pin MUST be monitored — sending commands while display is refreshing will corrupt it. Full refresh takes ~2 seconds with visible flicker; partial refresh is faster (~0.3s) but can cause ghosting after many partial updates — do a full refresh periodically. NOT suitable for animations or fast-updating data. Waveshare has gone through multiple hardware revisions (V1/V2/V3) with different driver chips — code for V1 may not work on V3. Use GxEPD2 library (Arduino) or Waveshare's Python examples (RPi). Operating temperature limited to 0-50°C — display may not update in cold environments. Tri-color (red/black/white) variant available but has ~15 second refresh time.

name: 2.13" 250×122 E-Paper Display Module (SPI, Waveshare Compatible)
description: 2.13 inch e-paper (e-ink) display module, 250×122 pixel resolution, bi-color black/white, SSD1680 driver chip, SPI interface with BUSY pin, 3.3V operating voltage, 48.55×23.71mm active area, 8-pin header, ultra-low power static display, ~2 second full refresh, partial refresh supported, 180° viewing angle, sunlight readable, popular size for name badges and IoT labels

specs.pinout: VCC, GND, DIN(MOSI), CLK(SCK), CS, DC, RST, BUSY

electrical.pinout: 8-pin: VCC, GND, DIN, CLK, CS, DC, RST, BUSY

designNotes: Popular e-paper size — good for name badges, IoT sensors, shelf labels. Same usage caveats as all e-paper: monitor BUSY pin, periodic full refresh to prevent ghosting, 0-50°C operating range. Waveshare has multiple hardware revisions (V1/V2/V3/V4) with different driver chips (SSD1675→SSD1680→UC8151) — always check PCB version marking and use matching library initialization. LILYGO T5 2.13" integrates this display with an ESP32 for a complete IoT e-paper solution. Use GxEPD2 library for Arduino/ESP32 or Waveshare Python library for RPi. HAT version plugs directly onto Raspberry Pi GPIO header. Partial refresh works well on V2+ for frequently updated elements (clock, sensor readings).

name: 2.9" 296×128 E-Paper Display Module (SPI, Waveshare Compatible)
description: 2.9 inch e-paper (e-ink) display module, 296×128 pixel resolution, bi-color black/white, SSD1680 driver chip, SPI interface with BUSY pin, 3.3V operating voltage, 66.89×29.06mm active area, 8-pin header, ultra-low power static display, ~2 second full refresh, partial refresh supported, 180° viewing angle, sunlight readable, largest common small e-paper size for dashboards and shelf labels

specs.pinout: VCC, GND, DIN(MOSI), CLK(SCK), CS, DC, RST, BUSY

electrical.pinout: 8-pin: VCC, GND, DIN, CLK, CS, DC, RST, BUSY

designNotes: Largest of the common small e-paper sizes — enough resolution for multi-line text, simple charts, QR codes. Same caveats as smaller e-papers: BUSY pin monitoring, periodic full refresh, 0-50°C range. Adafruit 4262 is a 4-level grayscale variant — richer visuals but different library initialization. Waveshare HAT version (SKU 12872) plugs directly onto Raspberry Pi. Multiple hardware revisions exist — check PCB version. Good for: shelf price tags, room signs, weather stations, IoT dashboards. Use GxEPD2 for Arduino/ESP32. The wider aspect ratio (roughly 2.3:1) is good for horizontal text layouts. Tri-color variant (red/black/white) available but has ~15 second refresh.
